reference_fidelity.notes: All screenshot-backed anatomies and much of the token system are represented, but geometry, density, avatars, icons, and signature details diverge materially.
evidence: submission/index.html:9-49 reproduces the 10px scale, dark palette, fixed shell, dock, list, board, file, chat, and modal structures from seed/DESIGN.md and seed/reference/screens/.
submission/index.html:125-141 implements Home, project dock, Message Board, To-dos/detail, Card Table/detail, Docs & Files, Chat, and Jump anatomy.

People are gradient initials and emoji/text glyphs rather than photographic avatars and consistent icons; 44px headings, 18px rows, repeated chat identities, and generic file tiles diverge from reference density and details.

visual_craft.notes: A coherent tokenized dark/light system and detailed micro-UI are offset by inline one-offs and inconsistent glyph-based iconography.
evidence: submission/index.html:9-50 defines coordinated color, type, spacing, radius, shadow, motion, focus, responsive, and reduced-motion rules.
Cards, pills, badges, trays, notifications, empty states, and board columns share consistent treatments.
Extensive inline styles and emoji/text icons weaken systematic polish.

product_model.notes: The required cast, launch narrative, tools, and key Basecamp concepts are rich, while relationships and shared-recording behavior remain shallow.
evidence: submission/index.html:91-117 defines 8 people, 5 messages, 10 todos in 2 lists, 6 active cards, three file types, and 11 seeded chat lines.
submission/index.html:125-140 uses All-access, pins, categories, assignments, due dates, steps, boosts, on hold, watchers, notifications, and My Bar terminology.
submission/index.html:130,134,137 reuse generic detail content; Done/Not now counts lack records, both home projects share one target, and no shared Recording/event model exists.

surface_coverage.notes: Every required screenshot-backed surface and several secondary layers are reachable, but optional/account-wide destinations are generic stubs.
evidence: The router at submission/index.html:141 provides ten substantive hash route shapes covering all required primary and detail surfaces.
submission/index.html:54-89 and 147-153 add Jump, five My Bar trays, notification sidebar, theme control, and filter empty states.
Activity, Calendar, Reports, Everything, admin, and all other unknown routes resolve to the same stubPage at submission/index.html:140-141.

functional_depth.notes: Navigation, filtering, todo completion, chat posting, notes, theme, and overlays work, while most create/edit/comment/move/delete flows are stubbed or inert.
evidence: Executable harness checks passed for message/todo filtering, empty states, chat escaping, persisted initialization, and todo persistence; submission/index.html:142-157 contains their handlers.
Twelve data-stub actions cover creation, adding tools/content, boosts, moving cards, and notifications.
Card filter, notification controls, category/sort controls, comments, detail steps, profile, bookmark/options, editing, and deletion have no handlers.

interaction_quality.notes: Live feedback and keyboard support are useful, but numerous inert controls, incomplete focus management, and no recovery make interactions only moderately trustworthy.
evidence: submission/index.html:31-32,145-157 provides focus rings, live-filter behavior, toasts, shortcuts, Escape handling, jump autofocus, and jump/sidebar trigger focus restoration.
submission/index.html:89 makes toast feedback a polite live region.
Jump/sidebar lack focus traps or background inerting, trays lack focus restore, and invalid IDs silently show the first record.

state_persistence.notes: Four narrow features persist, but most state is ephemeral and malformed/outdated storage has no validation, migration, reset, or recovery.
evidence: submission/index.html:111-115,142-154 persists todos, chat, theme, and My Notes; harness execution verified initialization and todo writes.
Completed expansion, Do Today, steps, comments, filters, and most displayed state are not persisted.
Harness tests confirmed malformed JSON aborts initialization and wrong-shaped todos/chat later throw; no reset or schema guard exists.

responsive_adaptation.notes: Two substantial breakpoints preserve core layouts with reflow and horizontal board scrolling, though mobile hides destinations and badges.
evidence: submission/index.html:48-50 reflows home, dock, content, jump tiles, message/file rows, metadata, chat, and sheets below 1000px and 700px.
submission/index.html:42 keeps Card Table usable through explicit horizontal overflow.
At 1000px My Bar hides its third through fifth destinations; mobile also hides row badges and reduces the notification pill to a dot.

accessibility.notes: Landmarks, skip/focus support, dialog labeling, keyboard shortcuts, announcements, and reduced motion are solid partial coverage with material naming and focus gaps.
evidence: submission/index.html:2,31-32,50,54-89 includes language, skip link, landmarks, focus-visible, labeled modal semantics, inert closed layers, and a live status region.
submission/index.html:147-157 supports autofocus, Escape, shortcuts, route focus, and focus restoration for jump/sidebar.
No focus trap/background inerting exists; trays lack dialog/focus management; several glyph buttons and comment textboxes lack accessible names.

code_architecture.notes: Reusable helpers and a dependency-free router make the prototype understandable, but all concerns are compressed into one untested mutable innerHTML monolith.
evidence: submission/index.html:118-157 has reusable avatar, escaping, breadcrumb, sheet, row, route, and event-wiring helpers.
The complete implementation is one 56,750-byte HTML file with CSS, data, persistence, routing, templates, and controllers mixed together.
There are no tests, modules, model validators, or explicit error seams; large string templates and full-main innerHTML replacement dominate rendering.

reliability.notes: The dependency-free script parses and escapes chat input, but malformed storage/routes and unescaped persisted todo fields create serious startup and safety failures.
evidence: submission/index.html inline JavaScript passed node --check; it has no external runtime dependencies.
submission/index.html:120,139,142 escapes posted chat before immediate and persisted rendering.

Harness tests confirmed malformed JSON and wrong-shaped stored data crash; submission/index.html:141 can throw on malformed percent encoding, and stored todo titles are emitted into innerHTML unescaped.

summary: A visually strong, broad single-file Basecamp prototype with faithful shell anatomy and several real persistent interactions. Its product graph and primary surfaces are convincing, but most creation/editing/commenting/movement/deletion controls are stubbed or inert, persistence is narrow and brittle, and the monolithic innerHTML architecture lacks production-grade validation and recovery.
